title: anthyllis vulneraria subsp. vitellina
intro: Anthyllis vulneraria subsp. vitellina, also known as kidney vetch or yellow vetchling, is a perennial plant in the legume family that grows in Europe, North Africa, and Western Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to treat wounds and skin conditions due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Anthyllis vulneraria subsp. vitellina include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and treat urinary tract issues, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of Anthyllis vulneraria subsp. vitellina include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used externally to treat skin conditions or internally to treat digestive and urinary issues.
